summary:
Claim: The mesiolingual and mesiobuccal pulp horns are most likely to be exposed during a Class II cavity preparation on a maxillary first molar.  
Evidence: - These pulp horns project significantly above the occlusal surface due to their steep angulations. - Anatomical studies show they are the highest-riding structures in maxillary first molars, especially the mesiolingual horn.  
Answer: A ✅  

(Note: Distobuccal and distolingual horns are lower and less angled, thus less vulnerable to exposure.)  
(498 tokens)
